name: feat-10-audit-log
initialPrompt: Enterprise customers are asking for an audit log so they can see who did what and when. I need: a searchable audit log page showing all user actions (logins, data changes, permission changes, exports), filterable by user, date range, and action type, with the ability to export the log to CSV. The log must be tamper-evident — entries should not be editable or deletable by anyone including admins. Also expose it via API so customers can pull it into their SIEM.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Tamper-evident audit log with API access

blocks.text: Provide enterprise customers with a tamper-evident, searchable record of all user actions
successCriteria: full-text search returns results in <2 s
API returns paginated log within SLA

blocks.question: How do we make log entries tamper-evident?

options.label: Append-only table with DB-level delete restriction

blocks.chosen: Append-only table with DB-level delete restriction
blocks.rationale: Sufficient for compliance; hash chain adds complexity without proportional benefit for our threat model.

blocks.title: Design audit event schema and append-only storage
acceptance: events table has no UPDATE/DELETE grants
schema covers actor, action, resource, before/after, timestamp
indexed on user_id, created_at, action_type

blocks.title: Instrument application to emit audit events for all tracked actions
acceptance: login, logout, data change, permission change, export events captured
events written synchronously in same transaction as action
no audit events dropped on error

blocks.title: Build searchable audit log UI with filters
acceptance: filter by user, date range, action type
full-text search on event description
results paginated with infinite scroll

blocks.title: Add CSV export for audit log (reuses export infrastructure)
acceptance: exports respect active filters
large exports go async with email link
exported file includes all schema fields

blocks.description: High-traffic tenants may generate millions of events per day, degrading query performance
blocks.mitigation: Partition audit table by month; archive entries older than 12 months to cold storage

blocks.question: What is the minimum retention period customers require — 90 days, 1 year, or 7 years?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same risk (high event volume hurting performance) but description expanded to include API consumer load; id changed. AC-13 fallback must map risk-event-volume to risk-event-volume-v2.

decoy.note: Genuinely-new block. Shares surface tokens 'audit' and 'schema/API' with task-audit-schema. A comment on task-audit-schema must re-attach to task-audit-schema (id preserved), NOT this new API implementation task.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-audit-api-design inserted second (shifts all subsequent positions); task-audit-api-impl added; risk-event-volume id changed. Naive positional scheme loses alignment at dec-tamper-evidence onward. Denominator is 7.
